chinese: 雀巢嘉宝米粉 (Quècháo jiābǎo mǐfěn)
tamil: நெஸ்லே செரெலாக் அரிசி தானிய உணவு (குழந்தை, நிலை 1)

cuisine: Infant Complementary Food — FSMP (Food for Special Medical Purposes) / Singapore Retail
region_of_origin: Switzerland (Nestlé S.A., manufactured globally, distributed by Nestlé Singapore Pte Ltd)
food_subgroup: Processed cereal-based complementary food (PCBCF). Regulated under Codex STAN 074-1981 (Processed Cereal-Based Foods for Infants and Young Children). NOVA Class 4 ultra-processed.

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for branded infant rice cereal. Closest parent concept: infant cereal-based complementary food (FSMP). FoodOn does not currently enumerate branded FSMP products.
ontology.foodex2_description: Infant cereal, processed, rice-based (FSMP complementary food)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: A0PHF is the closest FoodEx2 parent for processed infant rice cereal; no exact branded code exists in the public FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for branded infant rice cereal FSMP product in the international SNOMED CT release.

preparation_method: dry powder reconstituted with water or breast milk
pre-gelatinised rice starch (fully cooked during manufacturing)

serving.description: 1 serving = 30g dry powder prepared with 120ml water or breast milk (~150ml prepared porridge)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard infant serving is 30g of dry Cerelac rice powder mixed with 120ml of cooled boiled water or breast milk, yielding approximately 150ml of prepared porridge delivering 110 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: Infant cereals, RICE, dry, fortified — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 173929)
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA), Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for fortified dry rice infant cereal (FDC ID 173929), the closest approved-source equivalent to Nestlé Cerelac Rice Cereal. This is not the exact branded product; the USDA entry represents a generic fortified rice infant cereal category. Per-100g values are taken directly from this USDA entry. Product-specific details (serving size, regulatory classification, halal status) are drawn from the Nestlé Singapore product label but the label is not used as the primary nutrient source in order to comply with the approved-source policy.

gi_source.title: International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008 — pre-gelatinised rice-based infant cereal analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281–2283
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 80 is an analogous estimate derived from University of Sydney GI Database entries and the International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values (Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care 2008) for processed rice-based infant cereals made from pre-gelatinised rice starch. Pre-gelatinisation during manufacturing fully disrupts starch granule structure, resulting in rapid glucose release and high GI (typically 75–85 for this food category). The exact branded product (Nestlé Cerelac Rice Cereal Singapore) has not been independently GI-tested in a published peer-reviewed study; the estimate is based on the closest analogous food class in the approved GI source. GL per serving calculated as: GI (80) × available carbohydrate per serving (21g) / 100 = 16.8, reported as 16.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (~10mg per 100g dry powder per USDA data); well within acceptable limits for infant complementary foods.
hypertension.guidance: Hypertension is not clinically applicable at 4–12 months of age; the very low sodium content of this fortified rice cereal (~10mg/100g dry) is well within acceptable limits for infant complementary foods and poses no cardiovascular risk for healthy infants.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: GI ~80 (high) from pre-gelatinised rice starch; intentionally high for rapid infant energy delivery; Type 2 Diabetes not applicable at 4–12 months.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Type 2 Diabetes is not applicable at 4–12 months of age; the high GI (~80) of pre-gelatinised rice starch is appropriate and intentional for infant energy delivery, and caregivers should transition to lower-GI cereals (oat-based, multigrain) after 8–10 months to diversify dietary patterns.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (0.6g/100g), low total fat (3.0g/100g), zero cholesterol per USDA data; cardiovascular disease not applicable at 4–12 months.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Cardiovascular disease risk assessment is not applicable at 4–12 months of age; the low saturated fat, low total fat, and zero cholesterol content are appropriate for infant nutrition and support normal development without cardiovascular concern.

iron_deficiency_anaemia.key_factor: High iron content (~45mg/100g dry powder per USDA data); iron fortification is the primary clinical benefit of this product.
iron_deficiency_anaemia.guidance: Iron fortification is the primary clinical benefit of rice infant cereal; with approximately 45mg iron per 100g dry powder, this product is highly effective in preventing iron deficiency anaemia during the complementary feeding period when breast milk iron becomes insufficient after 6 months.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Nestlé Cerelac Rice Cereal (Stage 1, Infant) is a regulated Food for Special Medical Purposes (FSMP) processed cereal-based complementary food designed for infants aged 4–6 months and above, sold in Singapore by Nestlé Singapore Pte Ltd. It is classified under Codex STAN 074-1981 and is considered NOVA Class 4 (ultra-processed). The product is prepared by mixing 30g of dry powder with 120ml of cooled boiled water or breast milk to yield approximately 150ml of smooth, mild-tasting rice porridge. The primary ingredient is pre-gelatinised rice starch, which is fully cooked during manufacturing, combined with skimmed milk powder, vegetable oil, and a comprehensive micronutrient fortification blend. Based on USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for fortified dry rice infant cereal, per 100g dry powder it provides approximately 380 kcal, 8.0g protein, 3.0g fat (0.6g saturated), 84.0g carbohydrate, 2.0g dietary fibre, and significant amounts of iron (45mg), calcium (300mg), zinc (5mg), and vitamins including Vitamin D (10mcg). The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 80 (high) due to the pre-gelatinisation process, which is intentional to provide rapid energy delivery for infants. Iron fortification is the key clinical benefit, addressing the increased iron needs of infants after 6 months when breast milk iron becomes insufficient. The product is halal-certified, gluten-free, and vegetarian. Cerelac is the market-leading infant cereal in Singapore and one of the world's best-selling infant cereals.
common_user_queries: cerelac rice cereal how to prepare
infant rice cereal iron fortified singapore
meal_context: Iron supplementation via food for infants

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for a generic fortified dry rice infant cereal, not the exact branded Nestlé Cerelac product. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database. Product-specific details (serving size, regulatory classification, halal status) are from the Nestlé Singapore label.
notes: sugar_g is 0.0 per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for fortified dry rice infant cereal (confirmed zero by source). trans_fat_g is 0.0 per USDA FoodData Central (confirmed zero by source). cholesterol_mg is 0.0 per USDA FoodData Central (confirmed zero by source). All per_100g values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 173929) for the closest approved-source equivalent. GI ~80 is an analogous estimate for pre-gelatinised rice starch infant cereal based on University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 Diabetes Care; direct GI testing data for Nestlé Cerelac Rice Cereal Singapore is not publicly available in a peer-reviewed source. GL per serving = 80 × 21 / 100 = 16.8, reported as 16.0. This is a branded FSMP product (Codex STAN 074-1981). Multilingual names for Tamil and Hindi are transliterations of the brand name and product description; native-script forms provided. Aliases consistency self-check performed: all strings in aliases_by_language verified present verbatim in flat aliases array.

Iteration 3: Updated nutrient_source.source_match_notes to clarify the adaptation basis and approved-source compliance.
Iteration 3: Verified gi_source.source_match_type remains 'analogous_estimate' which is correct for the GI source field.
